title: Implement client connection and login flow
description: Implement the client executable entry point, argument parsing for server IP, port, and nickname, TCP connection setup, and initial LOGIN command transmission.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN ./client 127.0.0.1 8888 alice runs with server available THEN it connects and sends LOGIN alice; WHEN connection fails THEN the client prints a clear error and exits; WHEN login fails THEN the server reason is displayed; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:160
review_initial_requirements: Review CLI argument handling before adding interactive chat behavior.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the client does not implement auth, GUI, database, or offline behavior outside approved scope.

title: Implement client select loop for input and server messages
description: Implement the client event loop that uses select on standard input and the server socket so receiving and sending can happen interactively in one process.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the user types while connected THEN input is processed without blocking server messages; WHEN the server sends messages THEN they are displayed while the client remains ready for user input; WHEN server closes the connection THEN the client prints a notice and exits; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:162
review_initial_requirements: Review terminal input and socket read paths for blocking behavior.
review_regression_requirements: Verify select watches both stdin and server socket as approved, and that server disconnect is handled cleanly.

title: Implement user command mapping
description: Implement InputHandler behavior that maps normal text and slash commands to protocol commands. Keep parsing local and predictable so malformed private-message commands do not reach the server.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN a normal non-empty line is entered THEN MSG content is sent; WHEN /w bob hello is entered THEN PRIVATE bob hello is sent; WHEN /list is entered THEN LIST is sent; WHEN /quit is entered THEN QUIT is sent and the client exits; WHEN /w lacks target or content THEN a local format error is shown; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:174
review_initial_requirements: Review command parsing tests for spaces and empty input before wiring to sockets.
review_regression_requirements: Verify local command mapping matches the approved client command syntax and does not create undocumented commands.

title: Implement message display formatting
description: Implement Display behavior for server response types so system notices, group messages, private messages, user lists, confirmations, and errors are readable in the terminal.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN SYSTEM arrives THEN it is shown as a system notice; WHEN CHAT arrives THEN nickname and content are shown as group chat; WHEN PRIVATE arrives THEN it is shown as private chat; WHEN USERLIST, OK, or ERROR arrives THEN it is displayed with the correct meaning; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:183
review_initial_requirements: Review display output examples for readability without adding a GUI.
review_regression_requirements: Verify display covers all approved server message types and does not hide ERROR responses.

title: Review client module against approved interaction vision
description: Use a same-model sub-agent review to compare the delivered client connection, select loop, input mapping, display behavior, and error handling against the approved design.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN all non-review issues before this row are closed THEN run same-model sub-agent review against source-specific claim/evidence checks; WHEN gaps or overstated claims are found THEN append follow-up issues and REVIEW-02; WHEN no gaps remain THEN close the CSV.
review_initial_requirements: Verify all prior non-review rows are closed before running this review.
review_regression_requirements: Check that the client connects with IP, port, and nickname and sends LOGIN; Check that client select monitors stdin and server socket; Check that normal text, /w, /list, and /quit map to the documented protocol commands; Check that Display covers SYSTEM, CHAT, PRIVATE, USERLIST, ERROR, and OK with readable terminal output.
